ultimate_goal: Build a movement connecting stagehands with training, jobs, and community through an integrated ecosystem of labor management, skill development, and opportunity discovery.

this_week_focus.note: Finalize/de-duplicate the Deadhang business documents (dh-5) and close out taxes (dh-8); get the contractor agreement + MSA attorney-reviewed (dh-10); keep advancing Crew Blueprint content and Production Atlas research.

branches.name: Deadhang Labor LLC
branches.role: Solo contractor turning into labor staffing agency
branches.ultimate_goal: Establish Deadhang as a reliable labor sourcing and staffing entity that can contract large gigs and deploy teams of stagehands.
branches.current_state: Operating as an Arizona single-member LLC (Schedule C, cash basis, owner-operated) in live-event production labor. Foundation is largely in place: EIN, business bank account, website (live + SSL, with complete privacy/cookie/terms pages), business email, invoice system + a live gig/contracts/payments tracker, W-9 workflow, record retention, a Client MSA template, a drafted Master Independent Contractor Agreement, a City of Mesa business license, and full canonical documentation (companies/deadhang-labor/ — incl. a living current-state business plan, a document register, and SOPs). In progress: finalizing/de-duplicating the business-document set (dh-5), closing out taxes (dh-8), and safety documentation (dh-11). The IC agreement's remaining gate is Arizona attorney review (dh-10). Insurance is intentionally deferred until scaling (dh-9). Still to build: team-deployment capability (dh-3/dh-4). A print-on-demand merch line is being added (dh-12, no new LLC).
branches.critical_blocker: Independent-contractor agreement drafted — Arizona attorney review is the gate before engaging others; safety documentation and team-deployment capability still to build (insurance deferred until scaling).

branches.name: The Crew Blueprint
branches.role: Skill development and industry knowledge resource
branches.ultimate_goal: Be the first US-standard, gamified, ground-up learning system for American live-event field hands — teaching skills, context, and industry integration (general field work first, specialized disciplines and rigging later) without formal certification.
branches.current_state: Foundation complete and no longer blocked by software development. Vision, standardized course architecture, multi-AI content workflow, brand, and legal/authority philosophy (job-readiness, not certification) are all decided. Website shell is live (WordPress.com, industrial Extendable theme, staging-first, Coming Soon). DELIVERY DECISION (2026-07-18): a gamified, static, browser-based experience — NOT a traditional LMS. The discipline/pathway map becomes a skill tree; progress is stored client-side (localStorage), so there are no accounts, database, or backend to break (this sidesteps the instability that blocked the LMS). Mechanics start highly simplified (XP, streaks, progress, one interaction per node) and grow later. POSITIONING DECISION (2026-07-21): built to US industry standards and taught GROUND-UP — start with field-hand / ground-crew fundamentals (general labor, load-in/out, gear handling, jobsite safety & professionalism — the owner's real, lived expertise and the widest entry point); specialized disciplines and (eventually) rigging come much later. Rigging is the owner's personal goal, not the foundation. Aim: be the FIRST US-standard gamified training for the trade — Tech Crew HQ proved the format but is Australia-only (Australian standards). Primary work is still content, now ground-first: produce canonical packets, continue Stagehand Fundamentals, organize the discipline/pathway (skill-tree) library — then wrap packets in gamified modules. Canonical documentation lives in companies/crew-blueprint/.
branches.critical_blocker: Not platform anymore — the constraint is content-production volume: producing canonical course packets and the discipline/pathway library.

timeline.description: Complete resource library organization by discipline/pathway, launch foundational curriculum

blockers: Content-production volume is the real constraint (packets take time)
Discipline/pathway repository mapping still to be built out (this becomes the skill tree)
Gamified delivery chosen over an LMS; heavy platform (Laravel/AWS/accounts/DB) is off the table for now — start static + client-side

work_items.task: Define safety and content authority model
work_items.why: Establish trust framework before scaling training content deployment

work_items.task: Map discipline/pathway repository structure
work_items.why: Organize content logically for learner navigation across roles and skill levels

work_items.task: Build gamified static learning delivery (skill tree, client-side progress — not an LMS)
work_items.why: Chosen over an LMS: a static, browser-based gamified experience (localStorage progress, no accounts/DB/backend) is simpler and sidesteps the platform instability that blocked the LMS. Discipline/pathway map = skill tree; mechanics start highly simplified and grow.

work_items.task: Create foundational resources — ground/field-hand fundamentals first (canonical packets)
work_items.why: Ground-first library, built to US standards. HEAD START: Course 1 'Your First Work Call' Module 1 is COMPLETE — 5 entry-level lessons (what a stagehand does, load-in/show/load-out, work environments, the crew, being valuable) in structured JSON, QA-reviewed (inventory: companies/crew-blueprint/09_content_inventory.md). Lighting/video/rigging/hazard folders started. Remaining: finish/organize more ground modules; specialized disciplines and rigging later.

work_items.task: Ship first gamified MVP module — a ground/field-hand fundamental (static, client-side progress)
work_items.why: THREE PROTOTYPES BUILT (2026-07-22), climbing the build ladder. (1) Module 1 'Your First Work Call' — a playable single-file skill tree (rung 1: cards): 5 lesson nodes, 80% pass gates, XP/streak/rank, localStorage, no backend. (2) 'Dock Sweep' — first embodied 2D SCENE (rung 2): drag to look around a load-in dock, catch timed hazards + lead-calls, tap gear to learn. (3) 'Coil Line' — first TRUE 3D / WebXR scene (rung 3) and first SKILL-FIRST one: stand on the dock, drag to look around, coil a cable over-under loop by loop (a specific hand skill), with situational awareness ambient not the objective; a bad coil tangles into a bird's-nest = safe failure with a visible cost; Three.js inlined so it runs offline, 'Enter VR' on a headset. Plus (5) 'The Shop Floor' — the composable EXPERIENCE FRAMEWORK the lessons plug into: one walkable first-person anime/cel-shaded jobsite where each lesson is a module at a station; engine owns world/player(walk+look)/interaction(reticle+tap+VR ray)/HUD/progression(XP saved)/WebXR, lessons register via CB.registerModule and only touch it through a ctx SDK (spec: companies/crew-blueprint/scenes/00_experience_architecture.md). Anime aesthetic (toon shading + outlines) baked into the material layer so content inherits it. Verified in Chromium (walk/interact/complete/XP/save, 0 errors). FIRST LESSONS PLUGGED IN (2026-07-22): a private 'Lighting Hand: Truck to Trim' build layers 4 real lighting lessons as stations onto the same engine — Coiling, Pin the Truss, Hang & Make Safe (skip the bond and it falls), Fly to Trim (walk out of the drop-zone before calling the fly); XP/levels/save; safe-failure paths verified; delivered privately per doc 09 (carries lesson content). Proves the whole thesis: real lessons drop into the walkable anime engine. Next: iterate the lessons + deeper grab/manipulate interaction. Plus (4) 'Make It Safe' — second 3D atom (highest-value: step 4 of the lighting-hand workflow): hang a fixture (clamp/safety-cable/address/ground-check) then fly to trim; skip the safety cable and it falls = safe failure with a visible cost. Backed by research/05 (how Transfr/Interplay/skill-sims are built; learning science) confirming the Three.js web-first + WebXR-later stack and the learn-do-check-debrief loop. All smoke-tested end-to-end in Chromium (0 errors) and delivered privately + archived in Drive (Course Data Packages → Playable Prototypes); content stays out of the public repo per doc 09. DIRECTION SET: lessons are role-based end-to-end workflows taught via safe failure with real consequences; first full-workflow design = lighting hand truck-to-trim (companies/crew-blueprint/scenes/lighting-hand-truck-to-trim.md). Remaining: build the lighting-hand workflow atoms (safety-cable beat first) → iterate → deploy to the Crew Blueprint site.

work_items.task: Investigate training/content partnership leads
work_items.why: Rated leads captured in data/roadmap/connections.md (Tech Crew HQ — closest analog, lacks rigging; Academy of Live Technology/Rock Lititz; ETCP/ESTA; Bigger Hammer; Rugged Training; ITI). Outreach deferred: build the first gamified module (cb-5) first. See partnerships/ playbook + research.

branches.name: Production Atlas
branches.role: Live-event industry intelligence & opportunity-research platform (not a job board)
branches.ultimate_goal: Become the U.S. live-event industry's primary research platform — making employer discovery, vendor ecosystems, career pathways, opportunity mapping, and regional production intelligence structured, searchable, and actionable.
branches.current_state: Moved beyond defining its architecture. It is a static GitHub Pages app (no backend/login/database/payment/scraping) that reads manifest-driven, structured research packages; product is 'Production Atlas' in the festival-atlas repo, with research on the research-version branch. Settled: vision, architecture, branching, research methodology, public-safe data model, and validation. Application navigation (~11 pages) and research framework are ~95%. Department research ~85% complete (Staging, Rigging, Lighting, Audio, Video/LED, Power, Site Ops, Logistics substantially done; Scenic started, not finished). Remaining: complete Scenic, expand employer/opportunity datasets, finish normalized-date wiring, usability, and governance/docs. Canonical documentation lives in companies/production-atlas/.

branches.name: Mobile Operations (Travel-for-Work)
branches.role: Base out of the Midwest in the van and work steady live-event calls — relationship-first, tax-smart
branches.ultimate_goal: Establish a stable Midwest work base where the owner can vanlife/camp while working steady stagehand & live-event calls — landing first through IATSE relationships (Grand Rapids Local 26 / Indianapolis) and structuring the travel as documented business through Deadhang Labor LLC so it's tax-smart. A dependable personal income base that funds the rest of the ecosystem while living mobile.
branches.current_state: New plan branch (2026-07-22), digested from the owner's handoff. Two docs hold it: companies/deadhang-labor/16 (city ranking + IATSE outreach — a Grand Rapids Local 26 head-rigger connection makes GR the top landing zone, Indianapolis #2, Milwaukee/Kenosha backup, Chicago only for high-paying targeted calls) and companies/deadhang-labor/15 (travel/vehicle tax — deductible only as an active contractor traveling for documented, pre-booked business, not speculative job-hunting). No outreach sent yet; the draft message and decision rule are ready.
branches.critical_blocker: None — next action is reaching out to the Grand Rapids Local 26 and Indy IATSE contacts to gauge call volume before committing a landing zone.

timeline.description: Actionable immediately; gated only on the IATSE contacts' responses, not on capital or other branches.

blockers: Deductibility requires booking specific calls BEFORE driving (documented business, not speculation) — see doc 15
Choice of first landing zone depends on the IATSE contacts' answers on year-round call volume

work_items.task: Reach out to the Grand Rapids IATSE Local 26 head-rigger contact (draft message ready in doc 16)
work_items.why: A personal connection inside a local — especially a head rigger — beats cold-applying in a bigger market; this is the top landing-zone lead.

work_items.task: Ask the Indianapolis IATSE contact about year-round call volume
work_items.why: Indy may offer steadier year-round work; needed to compare landing zones against Grand Rapids.

work_items.task: Pick the first landing zone (Grand Rapids vs Indianapolis) from the responses
work_items.why: Decision rule (doc 16): clear GR path → Grand Rapids; clearer Indy volume → Indianapolis; both warm → GR for vanlife comfort + relationship access.

work_items.task: Line up concrete calls before the drive
work_items.why: Booked calls are both the income and the documented-business proof that makes the travel deductible (doc 15) instead of speculative job-hunting.

work_items.task: Confirm the travel/vehicle tax treatment with a tax pro (fold into dh-8)
work_items.why: Deductibility hinges on active-contractor status + documented business + the tax-home rule (doc 15); verify before relying on any of it.

branches.name: Homes for Hands (care seed)
branches.role: A light care ethos for the caravan — resources + mutual aid, no land/build/business
branches.ultimate_goal: Keep the Homes for Hands care ethos alive without building anything: point the caravan (the mobile community of traveling live-event workers) to the existing US resources that already solve travel & lodging for free or cheap, and cultivate mutual aid among crew — care and information, not a place we own or run.
branches.current_state: Reframed 2026-07-22. The physical Homestead build is RETIRED and land acquisition is off the table: the US van-life resource ecosystem (The Dyrt, Harvest Hosts, Escapees, Workamper, free campsites) already solves lodging & travel for the caravan at scale, free or cheap. No land, no build, no business, no framework to run. What remains is a LIGHT care seed — a curated 'live & travel free' resource guide + a mutual-aid / ally ethos (Roadie Clinic / Backline style) for crew who need help. Resources + allies live in data/roadmap/connections.md and partnerships/research/04.
branches.critical_blocker: None — deliberately light: a resource guide + care ethos, no capital, land, or build.

timeline.description: Can be done anytime; low priority. No dependency on capital or Phase 1 revenue.

blockers: Kept intentionally light — the risk is over-building this again, not under-building it

work_items.task: Curate a 'live & travel free' resource guide for the caravan
work_items.why: Point crew to the existing free/low-cost resources that already solve travel & lodging (The Dyrt, Harvest Hosts, Escapees, free campsites, Workamper) + where to turn when they need real help. First draft written: data/roadmap/homes-for-hands-guide.md (living; verify details). The useful, effortless version of the Homestead.

work_items.task: Cultivate the Homes for Hands mutual-aid / ally ethos
work_items.why: Connect crew who need help to allies (Roadie Clinic, Backline) and to each other — mutual aid among the caravan. Care, not housing we run.

work_items.task: Set the light care standard: what help is offered, and clear limits
work_items.why: Define what Homes for Hands is and is NOT — a resource guide + mutual aid, not a place, a business, or a promise we can't keep.

work_items.task: Keep ally relationships and the resource/leads list current
work_items.why: The Roadie Clinic is contacted; keep allies warm and the resources/leads in data/roadmap/connections.md up to date. Deferred/light (build-first).

flow: 1. Deadhang Labor LLC secures contracts and generates revenue
2. Production Atlas discovers and surfaces job opportunities
3. The Crew Blueprint trains stagehands in the required skills
4. Contractor Tools manage scheduling, communication, and payments
5. Contractors complete gigs through Deadhang deployment
6. Repeat: System learns from gigs, improves training, expands opportunities

north_star: Build the movement: a self-sustaining Labor → Training → Jobs engine that lifts up live-event crew — connecting them to skills, work, and community, and pointing them to the resources and mutual aid that let them live and travel well. (The physical Homestead build is retired: the existing US van-life resource ecosystem already provides the lodging/travel infrastructure; what remains is the care ethos — Homes for Hands.)

end_goal.destination: The movement + a thriving caravan, with a Homes for Hands care ethos — not a place we build or own.
end_goal.what_it_is: A self-sustaining Labor → Training → Jobs movement that lifts live-event crew, plus a light care seed: pointing the caravan (traveling crew) to the existing free/low-cost resources that already solve travel & lodging, and mutual aid for those who need help. Care and information, not capital — no land, no built home base, no business to run.
how_to_reach_it: Phase 1 - Build the engine: make Deadhang Labor, The Crew Blueprint, Production Atlas, and Contractor Tools into a working Labor → Training → Jobs flow that earns revenue and grows a community of contractors.

Keep it light (Homes for Hands): curate a 'live & travel free' resource guide for the caravan and cultivate mutual aid; the physical Homestead build is retired because existing resources already solve it.
Ongoing - Grow the movement: the engine keeps feeding people, training, and work into a community that looks out for its own.

phases.name: Foundation - Labor, Training, Jobs
phases.goal: Stand up a self-sustaining engine: reliable labor, skill-building, and job discovery that earn revenue and build a contractor community.

phases.name: Homes for Hands - the care seed
phases.goal: Keep the care ethos alive without building anything: point the caravan to the existing resources that already solve travel & lodging, and cultivate mutual aid. No land, no build, no business.

journey.start_label: Where you are now: a solo contractor earning through Deadhang Labor LLC, building the rest on the side.

milestones.title: Solidify Deadhang's foundation
milestones.summary: Get the business itself in order — legal structure, compliance, and a repeatable way to track gigs — so everything else is built on solid ground.
milestones.outcome: Deadhang is legally sound and every gig is tracked.
milestones.leads_to: A stable base you can safely scale from.

milestones.title: Land a steady Midwest work base
milestones.summary: Base out of the van in the Midwest and work steady live-event calls — landing first through IATSE relationships (Grand Rapids Local 26 / Indianapolis) and structuring the travel as documented business so it's tax-smart.
milestones.outcome: A reliable stream of stagehand / rigging-support calls from a chosen home market, funding the rest of the build while living mobile.
milestones.leads_to: A dependable personal income base that powers the whole ecosystem forward.

milestones.title: Get paid reliably
milestones.summary: Stand up the day-to-day tools — invoicing, payment tracking, and dependable scheduling — so money and time are never the bottleneck.
milestones.outcome: Invoices, payments, and calendar run without friction.
milestones.leads_to: Operations that hold up as volume grows.

milestones.title: Map the industry (Production Atlas)
milestones.summary: Grow the industry-intelligence platform (not a job board): a static, research-package-driven map of employers, vendors, departments, IATSE pathways, and geographic opportunity. Architecture and the public-safe data model are settled; the work now is research-corpus depth and usability.
milestones.outcome: A trustworthy, searchable map of the industry contractors can act on.
milestones.leads_to: A steady inflow of work to feed people into.

milestones.title: Teach the skills (The Crew Blueprint)
milestones.summary: Grow the training resource content-first: canonical course packets and a visual, workflow-based way for stagehands to learn the craft and how it fits the bigger picture. The authority/safety model is set; the next-gen platform is deferred until content justifies it.
milestones.outcome: People can level up their stagehand skills through the Blueprint.

milestones.title: Build teams & win bigger contracts
milestones.summary: Grow Deadhang from solo work to leading crews — recruit a contractor network and the coordination to deploy teams on larger contracts.
milestones.outcome: Deadhang can staff and lead multi-person jobs.
milestones.leads_to: The labor engine that powers the whole system.

milestones.title: Close the loop — the engine sustains itself
milestones.summary: Connect labor, training, and jobs into one self-reinforcing flywheel: work is sourced, people are trained, teams are deployed, and revenue recycles back in.
milestones.outcome: Labor → Training → Jobs runs as a self-sustaining loop.
milestones.leads_to: A self-sustaining movement — and a community that cares for its own.

milestones.title: Care for the caravan (Homes for Hands)
milestones.summary: No home base to build — the existing US van-life resource ecosystem already solves lodging & travel for the caravan, free or cheap. Keep a light care seed: a 'live & travel free' resource guide + a mutual-aid / ally ethos for crew who need help. Care and information, not capital.
milestones.outcome: The caravan travels and rests on existing resources; crew in need are pointed to help — care without a build.
milestones.leads_to: A movement that lifts live-event crew and looks out for its own.

thirty_sixty_ninety.title: 30 / 60 / 90 Day Operating Dashboard
thirty_sixty_ninety.summary: A near-term execution layer that keeps the roadmap linear while translating the current milestone into practical operating windows.

windows.label: Next 30 days
windows.goal: Close out Deadhang's document finalization and taxes so the administrative base is fully solid.
outcomes: Business documents de-duplicated and consolidated to one canonical set (dh-5)
Tax balance confirmed and payment arranged; returns amended if needed (dh-8)
Contractor agreement + MSA sent for Arizona attorney review (dh-10)
windows.guardrail: Do not engage contractors or scale to team work before the IC agreement is attorney-reviewed and insurance is in place.

windows.label: Next 60 days
windows.goal: Close the remaining reliability gaps in the contractor tool set (invoicing and payment tracking are already built).
outcomes: Backend scope validated against real MVP needs — no overbuilding (ct-1)
Calendar authorization renews reliably without manual refresh (ct-3)
Invoice generator + payment tracking confirmed stable (ct-2, ct-4 already done)
windows.guardrail: Keep tools narrow and reliability-first: don't add backend/enterprise features before the real requirement is validated.

windows.label: Next 90 days
windows.theme: Connect labor, training, and job discovery
windows.goal: Prepare the first usable loop between Deadhang Labor, Crew Blueprint, Production Atlas, and Contractor Tools.
outcomes: Production Atlas deepens its research corpus (Scenic + employer/opportunity datasets)
Crew Blueprint grows its canonical course packets and discipline/pathway library
Anti-sprawl controls keep skipped work visible without breaking the linear path
windows.guardrail: Do not turn the roadmap into a storage dump. Promote only public-safe, decision-ready work.
